---
title: "flat_fetch_updated_data.Rmd empty"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r development, include=FALSE}
library(testthat)
```

```{r development-load}
# Load already included functions if relevant
pkgload::load_all(export_all = FALSE)
```

# fetch_updated_data

Thanks to the `ambientweatheR` package,
we can access all of the weather data from our devices
in a standardized format. This makes use of the Ambient Weather API,
which we demonstrate below. Remember to set your API
key in the `.Renviron` file!

```{r}
#devtools::install_github("andrewflack/ambientweatheR")
library(ambientweatheR)
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)
```

First, we list our devices:

```{r}
list_user_devices() %>%
  flatten() %>%
  pluck("macAddress") -> device_id
```

Then fetch the data for the device:

```{r}
fetch_device_data(device_id) %>%
  pluck("content")
```

Easy peasy!

We define a function that fetches device data for an individual device:

```{r development-fetch_updated_data}
# Prepare the code of your function here
```

```{r function-fetch_updated_data}

#' Fetch Updated Ambient Weather Data
#'
#' Fetches device data from Ambient Weather for a date range. If `lookback` is
#' positive, data are fetched from `today - lookback` through today. Otherwise,
#' the function looks for the most recent date in `existing_db_path` and fetches
#' from that day forward (overwriting that day if it already exists).
#'
#' @param device_id Character scalar. Ambient Weather device ID.
#' @param lookback Numeric scalar. Number of days to look back from today.
#'   Use a positive value to fetch recent data; the default `-1` triggers
#'   fetching from the most recent date found in `existing_db_path`.
#' @param existing_db_path Character scalar. Path to existing parquet data
#'   (default: `here::here("data/ambient_processed")`).
#' @param api_key Character scalar. Ambient Weather API key. Defaults to
#'   `Sys.getenv("AMBIENT_WEATHER_API_KEY")`.
#'
#' @return A tibble with device data, sorted by `date_time`, with `device_id`
#'   appended as a column.
#'
#' @importFrom ambientweatheR fetch_device_data
#' @importFrom purrr pluck map_chr map_df
#' @importFrom dplyr as_tibble mutate arrange
#' @importFrom cli cli_alert_info cli_alert_danger cli_alert_success
#' @importFrom here here
#' @importFrom fs dir_exists
#' @importFrom arrow read_parquet
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' fetch_updated_data("C4:D8:D5:3B:4C:F9", lookback = 7)
#' }
#'
#' @export
fetch_updated_data <- function(api_key = Sys.getenv("AMBIENT_WEATHER_API_KEY"), device_id, lookback = -1, existing_db_path = here::here("data/ambient_processed")) {

  today <- Sys.Date()
  if (lookback > 1){
    # in this case, we want to fetch data starting from n days ago until today
    cli_alert_info("Fetching data from the last {lookback} days...")
    start_date <- as.POSIXct(today - lookback)
  
  } else {
    # in this case, we want to check the database for the most recent date we have data
    # for, and start fetching from there
    
    cli_alert_info("Checking most recent date in the database...")
    start_date <- as.POSIXct(today)
    
    if (dir_exists(existing_db_path)) {
      existing_data <- list.files(existing_db_path, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE, pattern = "\\.parquet$") %>%
        map_dfr(read_parquet)
      
      if (nrow(existing_data) > 0) {
        most_recent_date <- max(existing_data$date_time)
        # floor to that day, so it automatically overwrites that day and fetches all data from that day onward
        start_date <- as.Date(most_recent_date)
        cli_alert_info("Most recent date in the database is {most_recent_date}. Fetching data starting from {start_date}...")
      } else {
        cli_alert_danger("No existing data found in the database.")
        stop("No existing data found in the database. Please provide a valid path to the existing database or set lookback to a positive number.")
      }
    } else {
      cli_alert_danger("No existing database found.")
      stop("No existing database found. Please provide a valid path to the existing database or set lookback to a positive number.")
    }
  }

  cli_alert_info("Fetching data starting from {start_date}...")
  seq.Date(from = as.Date(start_date), to = Sys.Date(), by = "days") %>%
    map_chr(as.character) %>%
    map_df(
      ~ fetch_device_data(device_id, .x) %>% 
        pluck("content") %>% 
        as_tibble()
      ) %>%
    mutate(device_id = device_id) %>%
    arrange(date_time) -> output_df
  
  cli_alert_success("Data fetching complete for device {device_id}. Retrieved {nrow(output_df)} records.")
  return(output_df)
}
```

```{r tests-fetch_updated_data}
test_that("fetch_updated_data works", {
  expect_true(inherits(fetch_updated_data, "function"))
})
```

# dump_to_disk

Once we have the data, we can dump it to disk:
    
```{r development-dump_to_disk}
# You can prepare the code of the dump_to_disk() function here
```
  
```{r function-dump_to_disk}
#' Dump Ambient Weather Data to Partitioned Parquet Files
#'
#' Partitions a data frame by date and device, creates a year/month/day folder
#' hierarchy under `output_path`, and writes one parquet file per
#' `year/month/day/device_id` group. Returns a character vector of all file
#' paths under the output directory.
#'
#' @param df A data frame or tibble containing a datetime column and `device_id`.
#' @param proj_root Character scalar. Project root used to build output paths.
#'   Defaults to `here::here()`.
#' @param output_path Character scalar. Relative path under `proj_root` where
#'   data are written (default: `"data/ambient_processed"`).
#' @param dt_col Bare column name for the datetime column used to derive
#'   `year`, `month`, and `day`. Defaults to `date_time`.
#'
#' @return Character vector of full file paths written under `output_path`.
#'
#' @importFrom dplyr mutate distinct group_by group_walk select
#' @importFrom purrr pwalk
#' @importFrom fs dir_create
#' @importFrom here here
#' @importFrom arrow write_parquet
#' @importFrom glue glue
#' @importFrom fs path
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' dump_to_disk(ambient_df, dt_col = date_time)
#' }
#'
#' @export
dump_to_disk <- function(
  df,
  proj_root = here::here(),
  output_path = "data/ambient_processed",
  dt_col = date_time
) {

  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      dt = as.Date({{dt_col}}),
      year  = format(dt, "%Y"),
      month = format(dt, "%m"),
      day   = format(dt, "%d")
    )

  df %>%
    distinct(year, month, day) %>%
    pwalk(function(year, month, day) {
      dir_create(path(proj_root, output_path, year, month, day))
    })

  df %>%
    group_by(year, month, day, device_id) %>%
    group_walk(function(.x, .y) {

      .x %>%
        select(-dt) %>%
        write_parquet(
          .,
          path(proj_root, output_path, .y$year, .y$month, .y$day, glue("{.y$device_id}_ambient_data_{.y$year}{.y$month}{.y$day}.parquet"))
        )
    })

  return(list.files(path(proj_root, output_path), recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE))
}
```

```{r tests-dump_to_disk}
test_that("dump_to_disk works", {
  expect_true(inherits(dump_to_disk, "function")) 
})
```
  

```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
# Run but keep eval=FALSE to avoid infinite loop
# Execute in the console directly
fusen::inflate(flat_file = "dev/flat_fetch_updated_data.Rmd", vignette_name = "Fetching Data from Ambient Weather API", check=FALSE, open=FALSE, clean=TRUE)
```
